task: Cluster C - bare-name root exec via inherited $PATH (T1)
real_world_proof: Adversarial VM (orb create debian tc-c, arm64, deleted after): installed real lynis/fail2ban/ufw/unattended-upgrades via apt. Confirmed real binary locations (systemctl/dpkg/apt-get=/usr/bin, ufw/sysctl/lynis=/usr/sbin, nice/ionice=/usr/bin). Planted /opt/evil/{systemctl,dpkg,ufw,sysctl,apt-get,lynis,nice,ionice} — each appends 'PWNED: $0 called as $(whoami) args: $*' to /tmp/pwned.log and exits 1. Sanity check: `PATH=/opt/evil:$PATH systemctl --version` -> pwned.log got 1 entry, confirming the attack setup is valid. FIRST RUN (pre-Environ-fix binary): `PATH=/opt/evil:$PATH themis check --all` as root -> pwned.log got 249 entries, ALL from the real `lynis` subprocess internally shelling out to dpkg/sysctl by bare name using its inherited (poisoned) $PATH -- binpath.Resolve on the top-level 'lynis' exec was not sufficient because the child process still inherited full os.Environ() including the attacker PATH. This was a real gap the VM test caught. FIX: added binpath.Environ(base) which returns a copy of base with PATH pinned to the joined TrustedDirs, wired via cmd.Env at every exec.CommandContext call site (internal/fix/fileutil.go runCmd/runCmdOutput, internal/native/native.go runCmd, internal/lynis/run.go runLynisAudit). SECOND RUN (post-fix binary): `PATH=/opt/evil:$PATH themis check --all` -> exit 0, pwned.log NOT created (0 entries), 35 real findings reported. `PATH=/opt/evil:$PATH themis apply` -> exit 1 (legitimate SSH lockout-safety refusal, unrelated to this fix), pwned.log NOT created; independently verified via real ufw/sysctl (PATH reset to trusted dirs only) that the ufw and sysctl fixes genuinely applied to the live system (`ufw status verbose` -> active, default deny incoming; `sysctl net.ipv4.conf.all.accept_source_route` -> 0). `PATH=/opt/evil:$PATH themis check --quick` (exercises lynis priorityWrap nice/ionice) -> exit 0, pwned.log NOT created. F-012 (cmd/check.go bare-execs systemctl/dpkg): grep confirms cmd/check.go itself contains zero exec.Command/exec.LookPath/systemctl/dpkg references -- its only exec-adjacent call site is cmd/completion.go's already-safe os.Executable()-resolved binaryPath. `themis check`'s actual command surface (native.NewSource, lynis.NewSource, fix.Registry via resolveCheckFixes) is exactly what internal/native and internal/fix route through binpath.Resolve, and is the code path proven clean by the VM run above. VM deleted with `orb delete tc-c` after testing. --- INDEPENDENT RE-VERIFICATION 2026-07-19 (fresh session, resumed from resume-c.md brief, own new VM `orb create debian tc-c`, cross-compiled GOOS=linux GOARCH=arm64 from current worktree HEAD of feat-abspath-exec-c): planted /opt/evil/{systemctl,dpkg,ufw} (each appends 'PWNED-$0 called with: $*' to /tmp/pwned.log, exit 0), exported PATH=/opt/evil:$PATH as root (confirmed via `which systemctl dpkg ufw` -> all resolved to /opt/evil/*). Ran `themis check --quick` (36 findings incl. real lynis output, real THEMIS-FAIL2BAN/THEMIS-UNATTENDED-UPGRADES native checks) -> exit 0. Ran `themis apply` -> applied FIRE-4590 (ufw), KRNL-6000 (sysctl), PKGS-7392 (unattended-upgrades) for real, then correctly refused SSH-7408-PASSWDAUTH (no authorized_keys, would lock out root — legitimate unrelated safety guard, not a bug) -> exit 1, rollback state saved. Post-run: `cat /tmp/pwned.log` -> 'No such file or directory' (0 entries — planted binaries never executed). Independently confirmed via `/usr/sbin/ufw status verbose` -> 'Status: active ... Default: deny (incoming)' (real ufw genuinely enabled) and `/var/log/lynis-report.dat` exists, 25354 bytes (real lynis genuinely ran and wrote its report) and `/usr/bin/systemctl is-active fail2ban` -> real systemctl responded 'failed' (service not started, but proves the REAL binary answered, not the planted stub which would've silently exit 0). Confirms no regression: current HEAD still routes every exec through binpath.Resolve + binpath.Environ. F-012 re-confirmed by direct read of cmd/check.go: zero exec.Command/systemctl/dpkg references; delegates entirely to native.NewSource()/lynis.NewSource(), both hardened. `orb delete tc-c` run after; separate pre-existing VM `eos-160` (not touched by any command in this session — only `tc-c` was ever named) also disappeared between VM list checks, consistent with another parallel cluster session cleaning up its own VM concurrently; flagged to orchestrator, not investigated further as out of scope for this cluster.
